evidence.text: Benefits are thorough with flexible time off Good benefits

Thomson Reuters has many opportunities for employees, job opportunities, volunteering, paid time off and benefits, employee groups, matching giving, leadership opportunities and product courses Navigating Change and Employee Benefits
Time off, flexible working (hours/location), insurance Great Benefits, No investment people

Sprawling organization covering most major markets and verticals worldwide with an area of coverage and office location for practically everyone. Lots of reporting opportunities with good pay and benefits

I have been with the company for one year. In that time I have experienced respect for the individual, Camaraderie, and overall concern for the well-being of all employees at this company. They offer great benefits considering most companies' benefits are below the bare minimum. TR is always training and growing the knowledge of its workforce. They offer opportunities in other areas of the company. Over the course o...
company seems to care about work-life balance, recently added 'mental health' days off work life balance
Good work life balance and stability Good work life balance
Great work life balance, benefits and well paid. Good company/good work life balance
Very flexible work/life balance. Wonderful employees. Great work/life balance. Needs to stop reorganizing annually.

TR cares about its people and shows it in its approach to time off, benefits, and special events. It's not just all talk, which is very different from many other places. Communications from leaders is also really good - open, honest, practical, thoughtful, and positive. The job I have is fantastic - I am paid and respected for my experience, and my ideas are taking seriously. While it is a fairly flat organization s...

CEO level has great vision for the company met with action Positive cultural environment supported by efforts to continuously improve culture Work/life balance is good, flexible working environment Variety of professional development opportunities Great Culture & Great professional development opportunities balanced with yearly layoffs
Great people, lots of opportunity for career growth. Good People
Big, global org - so opportunities to work in different areas of the company. Good initial Covid response. Major Problems there! Longterm employees are heading for the hills!
Flexible, remote, âgoodâ pay, opportunities to move around, some great people internally and clients. Benefits are great. Room for improvement DEI
Large company; potential for promotion in other departments. Reference attorney position
- Kind and supportive team, frequent access to c-suite, collaborative environment - Good WFH and hybrid set up Great if you're okay with corporate structure
The benefits, my specific team is very collaborative, office is convenient to be, hybrid work environment A great work environment for the most part

Great Benefits. Legal services and marketing that works for the attorney who can take on more clients. Respected Industry leader where Teamwork is abundant. Very accessible and good management who help you meet goals! Excellent Teamwork and Commissions!

evidence.role: Current employee - Document Automation Specialist - IT
evidence.text: diversity-focused, customer-centered community of professionals Progressive, Inclusive Company
Outstanding earning potential & 100% remote work environment. Above average benefits, & supportive management. Fast paced environment with High Income potential &
The people are great despite living in fear of the possibility of being let go at any moment If you value stability and clear direction then TR is NOT for you

In my years working at TR, I've always been in awe of my amazing colleagues; they operate with integrity and professionalism, have great senses of humor, and are very inclusive. 2 out of my 3 immediate managers during my time at TR have all been great; ensuring that your family and your health come first. TR responded very well during the pandemic. Still, to date, no one is required to return to the office, and alth...
Great colleagues and flexible work schedule for remote work Job security driving factor to leave
Respected organization, great benefits and job security Great news org
good benefits / supportive environment from peers myPay Solutions / Creative Solutions
Really don't have anything to say. Great benefits
The work is hard and stressful. The pay doesnât line up with the amount of work especially during the busy season. Itâs awful. Low pay high stress

No training Stair step commission fight the system for every sale you make- lots of errors in what they pay. So complicated many dont even try to figure it out Management is only worried about making the month- not who they are selling to. Good benefits and so so pay

With any company it depends on the team leadership some are great and others not so much, common lay offs every two years, encourages education and increased skill set for work, but doesn't pay for it or give time off for it. Excellent flexibility and benefits
Underpaid employees along Management sets unattainable benchmarks Limited upward mobility Retainment and training not great Limited Upward Mobility and Underpaid

- Low base salary - Slow annual salary growth - Lack of career growth support/opportunity - Leadership/management not there - Office quiet drama (people are all worry to lose their job, this environment is super negative, competitive and toxic) - Training support = BS - You can't expect your leader or managers, CEO, etc. will tell you any truth, so you can position yourself ahead. - Annual goal is a nightmare, they...
Favoritism in the workplace No career growth No growth

Base salaries are terrible, and salary increases don't keep pace with inflation. The premium for exceptional performance is insultingly low (on the order of hundreds of dollars, not thousands), and is given as a one-time bonus, not an increase to your salary, so that salaries can be kept low. They require attorney editors to be in the office one day a week even though the job requires literally no interaction with o...
Compensation isn't as competitive as other large organizations Solid experience
- Pay is not great - Hard to move up so career growth is not easy Software Engineer

Drastic yearly restructuring with new accounts/territories. Very limited data available with this restructuring. TR is a premium product yet. technical support. is subpar Comp plan hasnât been understood for 3 straight years and gets more confusing . in 2022 Every sales call I was on started with technical support problems. Job is difficult to meet a 35% INCREASED quota with untrained technical support and a reduc...

Can struggle a bit with integrating into work. I didn't have a lot of prior training but I did have a lot of help. Alot (even HR related) was learn as you go. You also have to follow up a lot with HR when you have requests. Very friendly work environment I don't see a reason to leave

I'm concerned about the recent leadership changes at Thomson Reuters. The new leaders don't seem to have the employees' best interests at heart. They've eliminated incentives and micromanage employees, which makes for a toxic work environment. Product support

Most send a staggering number of emails instead of using TEAMS for a more efficient way to complete a task. EA community is not as warm and welcoming as suggested. Many try to micromanage the new EAs and tell them how to do their work even though Steve Hasker encourages people to challenge and bring their own perspectives for potentially more efficient ways of operating. They can be very demeaning and cliquish. I am...
They use plenty of cliches, "collaboration", "#teamwork" in practice it is not what they mean! High turnover
Too much bureaucracy Toxic office politics You have the autonomy because management never wants to stick their neck out for anything It's a giant, diversified company
Doesn't pay the top of the market Great Place to Work
favoritism and unclear objectives across divisions Fine lots of changes
Micromanaging. No trust from management. Just a number in paper What itâs like
Company is driven by twisted devotion to DEI and identity politics. The higher you are on the victim hierarchy the better your chances of hire or promotion. Horrible
Constant reorganizations, layoffs, etc. Raises are low and salaries are not competitive. Good work/life balance
Support is the achilles heel. Awful. They keep piling more solutions on us to sell every year and change 50% of our customer assignments every year. No work/life balance

High pressure, toxic sales environment. People take leave instead of vacation out of fear for losing their jobs. Tenure has no bearing, senior sales staff are as stressed as newer reps. Favoritism flourishes and unfair practices seem to go totally unchecked by HR. No work life balance

They are having issues keeping people at the company. Their salary is not as competitive. They did not give a raise this year to meet inflation. So technically I am making less that I was previous years. Even with the raise. They are not good at promoting. If the position is open you can apply, but just cause you have been in a role for so long is it enough to be promoted or create the next job level up to promote y...

1. Promotions take some time 2. You need to be patient to grow within the Product team at TR 3. Negotiate salary while joining as salaries may not grow at the rate you expect once you are working with TR Great company to work and very good work life balance!
